questions.prompt: A scholarship fund increases by 15% from $20,000. What is the new amount?
The ratio of students in program A to program B is 7:9. If there are 288 students total, how many are in program A?
questions.rationale: Total parts = 16. One part = 18. Program A = 7 × 18 = 126.

questions.prompt: A test has sections with different weights. What is the weighted score?
You have a 3-liter jug and a 5-liter jug. You need exactly 4 liters. What is the minimum number of pour/fill/empty operations needed?
questions.hint: You can fill a jug, empty it, or pour from one to the other.
questions.rationale: Fill 5L → Pour into 3L (2L left in 5L) → Empty 3L → Pour 2L from 5L into 3L → Fill 5L → Pour into 3L (1L space) → 4L left in 5L jug. That's 6 operations.

questions.prompt: What is the time complexity of this function?
questions.rationale: Single pass through the array with O(1) Set lookups → O(n) total.

questions.prompt: What is the next number in this pattern?
questions.rationale: Fibonacci sequence: each number is the sum of the two before it. 8 + 13 = 21.

questions.prompt: If you flip a fair coin 10 times and get heads every time, the probability of heads on the 11th flip is still 50%.
questions.rationale: Each coin flip is independent. Past results do not affect future probabilities.

questions.prompt: A snail climbs 3 meters during the day but slides back 2 meters at night. The well is 10 meters deep. On which day does the snail reach the top?
questions.rationale: After 7 days: net progress = 7 × 1 = 7 meters. On day 8, the snail climbs 3 more meters (reaches 10), escaping before nightfall.

questions.prompt: SQUARE is to 4 as HEXAGON is to ___
questions.rationale: A square has 4 sides; a hexagon has 6 sides.
